```bash
if [ ! -f /etc/aide/aide.conf ]; then
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE configuration file exists
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide/aide.conf"

if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Read all lines from the AIDE configuration file
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check if the line contains the keyword 'file' and is followed by a rule set
    if [[ $line == file* ]]; then
        # Append ',-a' to enable extended attribute checking for this rule set
        sed -i "s/$line/$line,-a/" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
done < "$CONFIG_FILE"

# Restart AIDE to apply the changes
systemctl restart aide
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers in order of strength
fips_ciphers="chacha20-poly1305@ssl aes256-gcm@ssl aes128-gcm@ssl arcfour16-challenge aes256-ctr aes192-ctr aes128-ctr 3des-cbc sha256"

# Check if the SSH configuration file exists and is writable
if [ -w /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    # Read current ciphers from sshd_config
    current_ciphers=$(grep -oP 'Ciphers\s*=\s*(.+)'" /etc/ssh/sshd_config)

    if [[ "$current_ciphers" != *"$fips_ciphers"* ]]; then
        # Backup the original sshd_config file
        cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak

        # Update the sshd_config with the FIPS 140-2 ciphers
        sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/.*/Ciphers '"$fips_ciphers"'/g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

        # Restart SSH service to apply changes
        systemctl restart sshd
    fi
else
    echo "sshd_config is not writable or does not exist."
```
fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Load system cryptographic policy
CRYPTO_POLICY=$(cryptsetup is-policy | grep 'is_policy' | awk '{print $2}')

# Check if SSH server configuration file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    echo "SSH server configuration file not found."
    exit 1
fi

# Read current MAC algorithms from SSH server configuration
CURRENT_MACS=$(grep '^MACs' /etc/ssh/sshd_config | awk '{print $2}')

# Check if any non-FIPS 140-2 validated MAC algorithm is used
if echo "$CURRENT_MACS" | grep -E '^(hmac-sha1|hmac-md5)' > /dev/null; then
    # Remove non-FIPS 140-2 validated MAC algorithms from SSH server configuration
    sed -i '/^MACs/ s/hmac-sha1//g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    sed -i '/^MACs/ s/hmac-md5//g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

    # Update SSH server configuration with FIPS 140-2 validated MAC algorithms
    echo "MACs $(echo "$CURRENT_MACS" | grep -E '^(umac-$(grep -v '^#' <<<"$CRYPTO_POLICY" | awk '{print $1}')$')"' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

    # Restart SSH server to apply changes
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    # If all MAC algorithms are FIPS 140-2 validated, ensure they match the system policy
    echo "$CURRENT_MACS" | grep -E '^(umac-$(grep -v '^#' <<<"$CRYPTO_POLICY" | awk '{print $1}')$' > /dev/null || {
        # If not, update SSH server configuration with FIPS 140-2 validated MAC algorithms
        sed -i '/^MACs/ s/^.*$/MACs $(echo "$CURRENT_MACS" | grep -E '^(umac-$(grep -v '^#' <<<"$CRYPTO_POLICY" | awk '{print $1}')$')")/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

        # Restart SSH server to apply changes
        systemctl restart sshd
    }
fi
```

```bash
mkdir -p /etc/gnome-session/smartcard

cat << 'EOF' > /etc/gnome-session/smartcard/lock-on-removal.gschema
[org.gnome.desktop.session]
lock-screen-on-logout=true
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > /etc/systemd/system/gnome-smartcard-lock.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "gnome-session-quit --login"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable and start the service to enforce the policy
systemctl enable gnome-smartcard-lock.service
systemctl start gnome-smartcard-lock.service
```

```bash
# Check if gssproxy package is installed
if rpm -q --queryformat='%{NAME}' gssproxy > /dev/null 2>&1; then

fi

systemctl stop gssproxy.service

systemctl disable gssproxy.service

# Remove any system units related to gssproxy
rm -f /usr/lib/systemd/system/gssproxy.service
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Set up GPG for signature verification
if ! grep -qE 'gpg: /etc/gpg/gpg.conf' /etc/sysconfig/rpm; then
    echo "gpg: /etc/gpg/gpg.conf" >> /etc/sysconfig/rpm
fi

# Ensure the rpm command checks signatures by default
if ! grep -qE '^--requireproto gpg' /etc/sysconfig/rpm; then
    sed -i '/^--requireproto/ s/^.*$/& gpg/' /etc/sysconfig/rpm
fi

# Set up a hook to verify signatures during installation
if [ ! -f /usr/lib/systemd/system/rpm-pretrans.service ]; then
    cat <<EOF > /usr/lib/systemd/system/rpm-pretrans.service
[Unit]
Description=Verify RPM Signatures Before Installation

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/bin/rpm --checksig %p

[Install]
WantedBy=rpm-install.service
EOF
fi

# Enable and start the hook service
systemctl enable rpm-pretrans.service
systemctl start rpm-pretrans.service
```

fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Check if profile-based configuration tool is available
if ! command -v authconfig &> /dev/null; then
    echo "authconfig is not installed."
    exit 1
fi

# Select and activate a supported authentication profile
authconfig --updateall --enablemd5 --enableshadow --useshadowpasswords=yes --enablepam --enablemkhomedir --enablelastlog --enablensswitch &> /dev/null

# Check if the configuration was successful
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Authentication configuration updated successfully."
else
    echo "Failed to update authentication configuration."
    exit 1
fi
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define the required login banner content
banner_content="This system is for authorized use only. Use of this system constitutes consent to monitoring of all activities. Any unauthorized access or activity may be subject to legal action."

# Check if the banner file exists and has the correct content
if [ -f /etc/issue ]; then
    current_content=$(cat /etc/issue)
    if [[ "$current_content" != *"$banner_content"* ]]; then
        # Update the banner file with the required content
        echo "$banner_content" > /etc/issue
    fi
else
    # Create the banner file and add the required content
    echo "$banner_content" > /etc/issue
fi

# Set the correct permissions for the banner file
chmod 644 /etc/issue

else

if grep -qE 'pam_unix.so.*try_first_pass' /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
    # Set the lockout interval in pam_unix module configuration

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define a function to check password strength
check_password_strength() {
    local oldpass="$1"
    local newpass="$2"

    # Calculate the number of differing characters
    local diff_chars=$(echo -n "$oldpass" | grep -o . | sort | uniq | wc -l)

    # Check if the difference is at least 8 characters
    if [ $diff_chars -ge 8 ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Update the PAM password policy module
sed -i '/password requisite pam_pwquality.so/,$ d' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
echo "password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3 minlen=8 difok=8" >> /etc/pam.d/system-auth

# Apply the new policy to all users
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    if [ "$user" != "root" ]; then
        chage --max-diff=8 "$user"
    fi
done

# Set the password strength check function as a post-change command
echo 'password_change_command=/usr/local/bin/check_password_strength' >> /etc/login.defs

# Create or update the check_password_strength script
cat << EOF > /usr/local/bin/check_password_strength
#!/bin/bash

if [ "\$1" == "\$2" ]; then
    echo "Passwords must differ by at least 8 characters."
    exit 1
fi

oldpass="\$(openssl passwd -1 "\$1")"
newpass="\$(openssl passwd -1 "\$2")"

diff_chars=\$(echo -n "\$oldpass" | grep -o . | sort | uniq | wc -l)

if [ \$diff_chars -lt 8 ]; then
    echo "Passwords must differ by at least 8 characters."
    exit 1
fi

exit 0
EOF

# Make the script executable
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/check_password_strength

# Define the maximum number of consecutive characters from the same class
MAX_CONSECUTIVE=4

# Function to check password quality
check_password_quality() {
    local password="$1"
    local char_classes="[:upper:][:lower:][:digit:][:punct:]"

    for (( i=0; i<${#password}; i++ )); do
        local current_char="${password:$i:1}"
        local class=$(echo "$char_classes" | grep -oP "[$current_char]" | head -n 1)
        if [[ -z $class ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        # Count consecutive characters from the same class
        local count=0
        for (( j=i; j<${#password}; j++ )); do
            local next_char="${password:$j:1}"
            local next_class=$(echo "$char_classes" | grep -oP "[$next_char]" | head -n 1)
            if [[ $next_class == $class ]]; then
                ((count++))
            else
                break
            fi
        done

        # If the count exceeds the maximum allowed, return false
        if (( count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE )); then
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# Read current password policy settings
current_policy=$(grep -oP 'password_quality = .+' /etc/security/pwquality.conf | cut -d' ' -f3)

# If the current policy does not match the desired setting, update it
if [[ "$current_policy" != "1" ]]; then
    sed -i '/^password_quality/ s/=.*/= 1/' /etc/security/pwquality.conf
fi

```bash
        # Count consecutive characters from the same class
        local count=0
        for (( j=i; j<${#password}; j++ )); do
            local next_char="${password:$j:1}"
            local next_class=$(echo "$char_classes" | grep -oP "[$next_char]" | head -n 1)
            if [[ $next_class == $class ]]; then
                ((count++))
            else
                break
            fi
        done

        # If the count exceeds the maximum allowed, return false
        if (( count >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE )); then
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# Read current password policy settings
current_policy=$(grep -oP 'password_quality = .+' /etc/security/pwquality.conf | cut -d' ' -f3)

# If the current policy does not match the desired setting, update it
if [[ "$current_policy" != "1" ]]; then
    sed -i '/^password_quality/ s/=.*/= 1/' /etc/security/pwquality.conf
fi

# Apply the password quality check to the system-auth file
for line in $(grep '^password.*required' /etc/pam.d/system-auth); do
    if [[ ! "$line" =~ 'pwhistory' ]]; then
        sed -i "s/^$line$/$line pwhistory=5/" /etc/pam.d/system-auth
    fi
done

# Add the password quality check to the system-auth file if it's not already there
if ! grep -q '^password.*required' /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
    echo "password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3" >> /etc/pam.d/system-auth
fi

# Apply the password quality check to the password-auth file
for line in $(grep '^password.*required' /etc/pam.d/password-auth); do
    if [[ ! "$line" =~ 'pwhistory' ]]; then
        sed -i "s/^$line$/$line pwhistory=5/" /etc/pam.d/password-auth
    fi
done

# Add the password quality check to the password-auth file if it's not already there
if ! grep -q '^password.*required' /etc/pam.d/password-auth; then
    echo "password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3" >> /etc/pam.d/password-auth
fi
```

    cp /etc/authconfig/authconfig.conf.bak /etc/authconfig/authconfig.conf
    exit 1
fi

# Restart services that might be affected by the change
systemctl restart sshd
systemctl restart gdm
systemctl restart sssd

#!/bin/bash

# Define the maximum number of concurrent sessions allowed per user
MAX_SESSIONS=10

# Loop through all users to set their session limits
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    # Check if the limit is already set for this user
    if ! grep -qE "^$user:" /etc/security/limits.d/*; then
        # Create a new limits file for the user if it doesn't exist
        echo "$user hard maxlogins $MAX_SESSIONS" > "/etc/security/limits.d/$user.conf"
    else
        # Update the existing limits file for the user
        sed -i "s/^$user:.*/& hard maxlogins $MAX_SESSIONS/" "/etc/security/limits.d/$user.conf"
    fi
done

# Apply the changes to the current session
ulimit -Hn $MAX_SESSIONS

# Get all local interactive users
users=$(getent passwd | grep '/home/' | cut -d: -f1)

for user in $users; do
    # Get the primary group of the user

    # Set the group ownership for each file and directory in the home directory

done

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Get all local interactive users
users=$(getent passwd | grep '/home/' | cut -d: -f1)

for user in $users; do
    # Get the primary group of the user
    primary_group=$(id -gn "$user")

    # Set the group ownership for each file and directory in the home directory
    find "/home/$user" -type f -exec chgrp "$primary_group" {} \;
done
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

for user in $(awk -F: '{if ($3 > 500) print $1}' /etc/passwd); do
    homedir=$(eval echo ~$user)
    find "$homedir" -type f ! -name ".*" -exec chmod 700 {} \; -o -type d ! -name ".*" -exec chmod 750 {} \;
done
```

#!/bin/bash

# Function to check and set file permissions
check_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    local current_perm=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")
    if [ "$current_perm" -gt 740 ]; then
        chmod 0740 "$file"
    fi
}

# Loop through all users including root
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    # Skip non-interactive users and the system account
    if [ "$user" = "root" ] || ! grep -qE '^.*:' /etc/passwd -s -w -B 1 "/home/$user/bash-session"; then
        continue
    fi

    # Check and set permissions for .bashrc, .profile, and .bash_profile
    for dotfile in ".bashrc" ".profile" ".bash_profile"; do
        home_dir=$(eval echo ~$user)
        if [ -f "$home_dir/$dotfile" ]; then
            check_permissions "$home_dir/$dotfile"
        fi
    done
done

# Check and set permissions for root's .bashrc, .profile, and .bash_profile
for dotfile in ".bashrc" ".profile" ".bash_profile"; do
    if [ -f "/root/$dotfile" ]; then
        check_permissions "/root/$dotfile"
    fi
done

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Function to check and set file permissions
check_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    local current_perm=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")
    if [ "$current_perm" -gt 740 ]; then
        chmod 0740 "$file"
    fi
}

# Loop through all users including root
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    # Skip non-interactive users and the system account
    if [ "$user" = "root" ] || ! grep -qE '^.*:' /etc/passwd -s -w -B 1 "/home/$user/bash-session"; then
        continue
    fi

    # Check and set permissions for .bashrc, .profile, and .bash_profile
    for dotfile in ".bashrc" ".profile" ".bash_profile"; do
        home_dir=$(eval echo ~$user)
        if [ -f "$home_dir/$dotfile" ]; then
            check_permissions "$home_dir/$dotfile"
        fi
    done
done

# Check and set permissions for root's .bashrc, .profile, and .bash_profile
for dotfile in ".bashrc" ".profile" ".bash_profile"; do
    if [ -f "/root/$dotfile" ]; then
        check_permissions "/root/$dotfile"
    fi
done
```

# Check if the system is RHEL 8
if [[ "$OSTYPE" != "linux-gnu" || ! -f /etc/redhat-release ]]; then
    echo "This script should only be run on a RHEL 8 system."
    exit 1
fi

# Check for existing configuration in grub2
GRUB_CONFIG="/etc/grub2/grub.cfg"
if grep -qE '^\s*kernel\W+.*\W+page_table_isolation=1' "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
    echo "Kernel Page-Table Isolation is already enabled."
else
    # Enable Kernel Page-Table Isolation in grub2
    sed -i '/^\(\s*\)\(linux\s\+/ { s/$/ page_table_isolation=1/; }' "$GRUB_CONFIG"
fi

# Update grub2 to apply changes
grub2-mkconfig -o /etc/grub2/grub.cfg

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Check if /etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf exists and modify it
if [ ! -f "/etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf" ]; then
    echo "Creating /etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf"
    touch /etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf
fi

# Add or update the configuration to disable legacy virtual syscall mechanism
grep -qxF 'virtualization=none' /etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf || {
    echo "virtualization=none" >> /etc/grub2/dynamic/kdump.conf
}

# Update grub2 configuration for all installed kernels
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="virtualization=none"

# Ensure the setting is applied to new kernels automatically
echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="virtualization=none"' | tee -a /etc/default/grub

# Reconfigure grub2 to apply changes
grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

REMOTE_CERT="/etc/pki/tls/certs/remote_server.crt"

# Ensure the remote certificate exists
if [ ! -f "$REMOTE_CERT" ]; then
    echo "Remote certificate not found at $REMOTE_CERT"
    exit 1
fi

# Configure the system logging service to use certificate-based authentication
cat <<EOF > /etc/sysconfig/journald.conf.d/remote-auth.conf
SystemLogServer="tcp://$REMOTE_LOG_SERVER:$REMOTE_LOG_PORT"
SystemLogDirectory="/var/log/journal"
SystemLogRateLimitInterval=0
SystemLogRateLimitBurst=100
SystemLogSocketName=journald
SyslogFacility=LOG_USER
Compress=on
Color=auto
KeepDynamic=true
ForwardToSyslog=no
# Enable certificate-based authentication
RemoteAuth=yes
RemoteCertificate="$REMOTE_CERT"
EOF

# Restart the system logging service to apply changes
systemctl restart systemd-journald

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GnuTLS is installed
if ! rpm -q gnutls-utils; then
    yum install -y gnutls-utils
fi

# Configure journald to use TLS for remote logging
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/remote-logging.conf
[Remote]
SystemdJournalSendOverTLS=yes
SystemdJournalTLSVerifyPeer=no
SystemdJournalTLSClientCertificate=/etc/ssl/certs/journal-ca.crt
SystemdJournalTLSClientKey=/etc/ssl/private/journal.key
SystemdJournalTLSCAInfo=/etc/ssl/certs/
EOF

# Create necessary SSL files if they do not exist
if [ ! -f /etc/ssl/certs/journal-ca.crt ]; then
    openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout /etc/ssl/private/journal.key -out /etc/ssl/certs/journal-ca.crt -subj "/C=US/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/CN=Journal CA"
fi

# Enable and start the journald service with the new configuration
systemctl enable --now systemd-journald

```bash
if ! rpm -q gnutls-utils; then

fi

# Configure journald to use TLS for remote logging
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/remote-logging.conf
[Remote]

SystemdJournalTLSCAInfo=/etc/ssl/certs/
EOF

# Create necessary SSL files if they do not exist
if [ ! -f /etc/ssl/certs/journal-ca.crt ]; then
    openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout /etc/ssl/private/journal.key -out /etc/ssl/certs/journal-ca.crt -subj "/C=US/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/CN=Journal CA"
fi

# Enable and start the journald service with the new configuration
systemctl enable --now systemd-journald
```

#!/bin/bash

# Configure system logging to capture authentication, privileged-authentication, and daemon activity
LOG_DIR="/var/log"
AUTH_LOG="$LOG_DIR/secure.log"
DAEMON_LOG="$LOG_DIR/messages"

# Create log directories if they do not exist
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" 2>/dev/null

# Configure rsyslog to route messages to the appropriate logs
cat <<EOF > /etc/rsyslog.conf
$ModLoad imkern
$ModLoad immark
$ModLoad imuxsock
$ModLoad imtcp
$ModLoad imudp
$ModLoad imstats
$ModLoad imfreespace

# Log authentication and privileged-authentication messages to secure.log
local0.* /var/log/secure.log

# Log daemon messages to messages log
daemon.* $DAEMON_LOG

# Start rsyslogd with the new configuration
systemctl restart rsyslogd
EOF

# Ensure rsyslog is enabled on boot
systemctl enable rsyslogd

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Configure system logging to capture authentication, privileged-authentication, and daemon activity
LOG_DIR="/var/log"
AUTH_LOG="$LOG_DIR/secure.log"
DAEMON_LOG="$LOG_DIR/messages"

# Create log directories if they do not exist
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" 2>/dev/null

# Configure rsyslog to route messages to the appropriate logs
cat <<EOF > /etc/rsyslog.conf
$ModLoad imkern
$ModLoad immark
$ModLoad imuxsock
$ModLoad imtcp
$ModLoad imudp
$ModLoad imstats
$ModLoad imfreespace

# Log authentication and privileged-authentication messages to secure.log
local0.* /var/log/secure.log

# Log daemon messages to messages log
daemon.* $DAEMON_LOG

# Start rsyslogd with the new configuration
systemctl restart rsyslogd
EOF

# Ensure rsyslog is enabled on boot
systemctl enable rsyslogd
```

# Check if the sysctl is already set to ignore broadcast/multicast ICMP echo requests
if ! grep -qE '^(net\.ipv4\.icmp\.ignore_bcast_redirects|net\.ipv4\.icmp\.ignore_multicast_redirects)' /etc/sysctl.conf; then

    echo "net.ipv4.icmp.ignore_multicast_redirects = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.conf
fi

# Apply the sysctl settings immediately
sysctl -p

# Create or update a systemd service to ensure the sysctl settings persist on reboot
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/ignore-icmp-broadcast.service
[Unit]
Description=Ignore ICMP Broadcast Redirects

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp.ignore_bcast_redirects=1
ExecStart=/bin/sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp.ignore_multicast_redirects=1

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable the systemd service to start on boot
systemctl enable ignore-icmp-broadcast.service

# Remove ATM module from initramfs if present
if grep -q 'atm' /etc/initramfs-tools/conf.d/modules; then
    sed -i '/^atm/d' /etc/initramfs-tools/conf.d/modules
fi

# Blacklist ATM module in sysctl.conf to prevent loading on boot

    echo 'net.atm = 0' >> /etc/sysctl.conf

        echo 'modprobe -b atm' >> /etc/rc.local
    fi
fi

# Update sysctl settings immediately

# Ensure ATM module is not loaded by default
echo 'blacklist atm' > /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-atm.conf

# Create a systemd service to prevent manual loading
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/blockatm.service
[Unit]
Description=Block ATM Module Loading

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/false

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

systemctl enable blockatm.service

```bash
fi
```

systemctl disable bluetooth.service

    CURRENT_MOUNT_OPTIONS=$(mount | grep "$BOOT_PARTITION" | awk '{print $4}' | tr ',' ' ')

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define an array of non-root local partitions
non_root_partitions=($(lsblk -no PKNAME | grep -vv '/dev/root$' | grep -v '^/'))

# Loop through each partition and adjust the mount options
for partition in "${non_root_partitions[@]}"; do
    # Get the current mount options for the partition
    current_options=$(mount | grep "$partition" | awk '{print $4}')

    # Check if 'nodev' is already in the mount options
    if [[ ! "$current_options" =~ (nodev) ]]; then
        # If not, add it to the existing options and update the /etc/fstab file
        new_options="${current_options},nodev"
        echo "mounting $partition with nodev option"
        echo "$partition $new_options" >> /etc/fstab
    else
        echo "nodev option already set for $partition"
    fi
done

# Apply the changes to the running system
mount -a

# Check if SELinux is enabled
if ! grep -qE 'SELINUX=.*-enforcing' /etc/selinux/config; then
    echo "SELinux is not configured to enforce policies."
    exit 1
fi

# Install the file access policy daemon software (AppArmor)
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null; then
    yum install -y apparmor
fi

# Load AppArmor profiles
for profile in /etc/apparmor.d/*; do
    aa-enforce $profile || true
done

# Enable and start the AppArmor service
systemctl enable --now apparmor.service

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Check if SELinux is enabled
if ! grep -qE 'SELINUX=.*-enforcing' /etc/selinux/config; then
    echo "SELinux is not configured to enforce policies."
    exit 1
fi

# Install the file access policy daemon software (AppArmor)
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null; then
    yum install -y apparmor
fi

# Load AppArmor profiles
for profile in /etc/apparmor.d/*; do
    aa-enforce $profile || true
done

# Enable and start the AppArmor service
systemctl enable --now apparmor.service
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define the maximum polling interval for NTP servers
MAX_INTERVAL=64

# Get the current list of NTP servers from /etc/ntp.conf
current_servers=$(grep -oP 'server \K[^ ]+' /etc/ntp.conf)

# Loop through each server and ensure it has a maxpoll directive with the defined interval
for server in $current_servers; do
    # Check if the server already has a maxpoll directive with the correct value
    if ! grep -q "maxpoll $MAX_INTERVAL" "/etc/ntp.conf"; then
        # Add the maxpoll directive to the server line
        sed -i "s|^server $server|server $server maxpoll $MAX_INTERVAL|" /etc/ntp.conf
    fi
done

# Ensure that the ntpd service is restarted to apply the changes
systemctl restart ntpd

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Define the list of SSH-related files and directories to check
SSH_FILES=(~/.ssh/authorized_keys ~/.ssh/config)

# Function to check file permissions
check_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -w "$file" ] || [ -x "$file" ]; then
        echo "Insecure permissions on $file: world-writable or executable"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check ownership
check_ownership() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ "$(stat -c '%U' "$file")" != "$(whoami)" ]; then
        echo "Insecure ownership on $file: not owned by $(whoami)"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Loop through each SSH-related file and check permissions and ownership
for file in "${SSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -e "$file" ]; then
        check_permissions "$file"
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            chmod go-wx "$file"
        fi

        check_ownership "$file"
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            chown "$(whoami)" "$file"
        fi
    else
        touch "$file" && chmod 600 "$file" && chown "$(whoami)" "$file"
    fi
done

# Set up a cron job to run this script at boot time
CRON_JOB="0 * * * * /bin/bash -c '/usr/bin/ssh-check.sh'"
if ! crontab -l | grep -qE "^$CRON_JOB"; then
    (crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "$CRON_JOB") | crontab -
fi

# Create a systemd service to enforce the SSH check at boot time
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/ssh-check.service
[Unit]
Description=SSH Check Service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c '/usr/bin/ssh-check.sh'

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable and start the service
systemctl enable ssh-check.service
systemctl start ssh-check.service

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define the list of SSH-related files and directories to check
SSH_FILES=(~/.ssh/authorized_keys ~/.ssh/config)

# Function to check file permissions
check_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -w "$file" ] || [ -x "$file" ]; then
        echo "Insecure permissions on $file: world-writable or executable"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check ownership
check_ownership() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ "$(stat -c '%U' "$file")" != "$(whoami)" ]; then
        echo "Insecure ownership on $file: not owned by $(whoami)"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Loop through each SSH-related file and check permissions and ownership
for file in "${SSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -e "$file" ]; then
        check_permissions "$file"
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            chmod go-wx "$file"
        fi

        check_ownership "$file"
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            chown "$(whoami)" "$file"
        fi
    else
        touch "$file" && chmod 600 "$file" && chown "$(whoami)" "$file"
    fi
done

# Set up a cron job to run this script at boot time
CRON_JOB="0 * * * * /bin/bash -c '/usr/bin/ssh-check.sh'"
if ! crontab -l | grep -qE "^$CRON_JOB"; then
    (crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "$CRON_JOB") | crontab -
fi

# Create a systemd service to enforce the SSH check at boot time
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/ssh-check.service
[Unit]
Description=SSH Check Service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c '/usr/bin/ssh-check.sh'

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable and start the service
systemctl enable ssh-check.service
systemctl start ssh-check.service
```

# Check if SSH server configuration file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    echo "sshd_config file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original sshd_config
cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak

# Read current SSH server configuration
while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ $line == "LoginGraceTime" ]]; then
        # Set LoginGraceTime to 1 minute
        sed -i "s/^LoginGraceTime.*/LoginGraceTime 60/" /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    elif [[ $line == "PrintLastLogins" ]]; then
        # Check if PrintLastLogins is already enabled
        if [[ $line == "yes" ]]; then
            echo "PrintLastLogins is already enabled."
            exit 0
        else
            # Enable PrintLastLogins
            sed -i "s/^PrintLastLogins.*/PrintLastLogins yes/" /etc/ssh/sshd_config
        fi
    fi
done < /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

# Define the maximum amount of data and time for key renegotiation
MAX_DATA=524288000 # 512MB in bytes
MAX_TIME=3600      # 1 hour in seconds

# Check if SSH configuration file exists, create it if not
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

# Read the current SSH configuration and check for existing settings
current_max_data=$(grep 'MaxAuthrenegotiateWindow' /etc/ssh/sshd_config | cut -d' ' -f3)
current_max_time=$(grep 'MaxAuthrenegotiateInterval' /etc/ssh/sshd_config | cut -d' ' -f3)

# Update the SSH configuration with new settings if they are not already set
if [ "$current_max_data" != "$MAX_DATA" ]; then
    sed -i '/^MaxAuthrenegotiateWindow/s/.*/MaxAuthrenegotiateWindow '$MAX_DATA'/g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

if [ "$current_max_time" != "$MAX_TIME" ]; then
    sed -i '/^MaxAuthrenegotiateInterval/s/.*/MaxAuthrenegotiateInterval '$MAX_TIME'/g' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

# Restart SSH service to apply the changes
systemctl restart sshd

done

auditctl -R -

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Load existing audit rules if they exist
if [ -f /etc/audit/rules.d/zz-local-audit-rules.rules ]; then
    auditctl -l | grep -q 'type=AVC' && exit 0
fi

# Create a new file for local audit rules
touch /etc/audit/rules.d/zz-local-audit-rules.rules

# Write the audit rule to monitor failed truncate attempts by users with UIDs >= 1000
echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b32,b64 -S truncate -F key=access' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/zz-local-audit-rules.rules

# Load the new audit rules
auditctl -r /etc/audit/rules.d/zz-local-audit-rules.rules

# Ensure the audit daemon is running and configured to load local rules
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl start auditd

else
    AUDIT_RULE="audit(-k module_load) -a always,exit"

fi

echo "$AUDIT_RULE" | tee /etc/audit/rules.d/50-module-loading.rules

```bash
#!/bin/bash

    AUDIT_RULE="audit(-k module_load) -a always,exit -F arch=b32"
else
    AUDIT_RULE="audit(-k module_load) -a always,exit"

if ! auditctl -s | grep -qE "^-a\+\(always\|never\),\(exit\|alert\)\s+-F\s+arch=[bB]32"; then
    echo "$AUDIT_RULE" | auditctl -w /sys/module/ -p warx -k module_load
```

service auditd reload

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define the audit rules for monitoring failed authentication and login/logout events
AUDIT_RULES="
-w /var/log/secure -p war -k logins
-w /var/log/auth.log -p war -k logins
"

# Check if the audit subsystem is enabled
if ! auditctl -l | grep -q 'auditd'; then
    echo "Audit subsystem is not running. Starting auditd..."
    systemctl start auditd
fi

# Apply the audit rules
echo "$AUDIT_RULES" | auditctl -w /var/log/secure -p war -k logins
echo "$AUDIT_RULES" | auditctl -w /var/log/auth.log -p war -k logins

# Ensure the rules persist across reboots by adding them to the audit configuration file
cat >> /etc/audit/audit.rules << EOF
-w /var/log/secure -p war -k logins
-w /var/log/auth.log -p war -k logins
EOF

# Update the audit configuration and reload the rules
auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules
systemctl restart auditd

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Check if auditctl is already configured to switch to single-user mode on disk full
if ! auditctl -s | grep -q 'action=exit\|action=exec'; then
    # Configure auditctl to switch to single-user mode when disk space is below 10%
    auditctl -w /var/log/audit/audit.log -p warx -k disk-full
    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,arch=b64,syscall=write,subsystem=file,action=exit,cond="field=field=inode=/var/log/audit/audit.log,size>1000000"
    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,arch=b64,syscall=write,subsystem=file,action=exec,cond="field=field=inode=/var/log/audit/audit.log,size>1000000",arg1="/sbin/telinit 1"
fi

# Ensure the audit configuration persists across reboots
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/audit-disk-full.service
[Unit]
Description=Switch to single-user mode on disk full for audit records

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/auditctl -w /var/log/audit/audit.log -p warx -k disk-full
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/auditctl -a -F arch=b32,arch=b64,syscall=write,subsystem=file,action=exit,cond="field=field=inode=/var/log/audit/audit.log,size>1000000"
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/auditctl -a -F arch=b32,arch=b64,syscall=write,subsystem=file,action=exec,cond="field=field=inode=/var/log/audit/audit.log,size>1000000",arg1="/sbin/telinit 1"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Enable the service to start on boot
systemctl enable audit-disk-full.service

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define the audit log directory and threshold percentage
AUDIT_LOG_DIR="/var/log/audit"
THRESHOLD=25

# Calculate the free space in bytes
FREE_SPACE=$(df -k "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -1 | awk '{print $4}')

# Get the total space in bytes
TOTAL_SPACE=$(df -k "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -1 | awk '{print $2}')

# Calculate the threshold in bytes
THRESHOLD_BYTES=$((TOTAL_SPACE * THRESHOLD / 100))

# Check if free space is below the threshold
if [ "$FREE_SPACE" -lt "$THRESHOLD_BYTES" ]; then
    # Set the low-space notification action to 'mail' with a custom message
    AUDIT的低空间通知动作='mail'
    AUDIT的低空间通知消息="Warning: Free disk space in $AUDIT_LOG_DIR is below 25%. Action: $AUDIT的低空间通知动作"
    auditctl -w "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" -p war -k low_space
else
    # If above threshold, remove the low-space notification action
    auditctl -d -w "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" -p war -k low_space
fi

# Ensure the audit configuration is persistent across reboots
echo "auditctl -w $AUDIT_LOG_DIR -p war -k low_space" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
